stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • A tagine is a cooking vessel that originated in North Africa during the medieval era.
  • The tagine's shallow, circular base helps to braise food slowly.
  • The tagine's tall, conical lid helps to trap steam and return condensed moisture to the dish.
  • Paula Wolfert is a cook and culinary historian.
  • Her book The Food of Morocco (2011) traces the history of the tagine.
stem: The student wants to describe the tagine's shape. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Paula Wolfert's 2011 book, The Food of Morocco, traces the history of the cooking vessel.
Able to braise food slowly and trap steam efficiently, the tagine is the subject of Paula Wolfert's 2011 book.
A tagine is a cooking vessel with a shallow, circular base and a tall, conical lid.
The design of a tagine, a type of cooking vessel that originated in North Africa during the medieval era, helps the vessel braise food slowly and trap steam.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It summarizes the information that describes the tagine's shape from the second and third bullet points.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice doesn't describe the shape of a tagine. Choice B is incorrect. This choice doesn't describe the shape of a tagine, just some of its features. Choice D is incorrect. This choice doesn't describe the shape of a tagine, only some of its benefits or functions.
